verifier_values.criteria: States that SteelTreeSolutions does not have an employee/employer relationship with Sweetmarch Building Partners
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: Sweetmarch Building Partners and SteelTreeSolutions have a contractor/subcontractor relationship.  In "Harris v. Atlantic Richfield Co.docx" the court held that the tort of breach of contract in violation of public policy does not apply outside of the employer/employee relationship and does not apply to commercial contracts. See "Harris v. Atlantic Richfield Co.docx" at page 10.  In its opinion the court noted that "[t]he parties have not cited nor has our research disclosed case law recognizing a Tameny cause of action outside the employment relationship. Several cases suggest such cause of action does not exist." See Id. at page 3. The Court also cited " In this case STS was a subcontractor, not an employee of SBP, therefore STS does not have a valid claim for breach of contract in violation of public policy.
